short_name: Personalized Brain Stimulation to Treat Chronic Concussive Symptoms
title: Personalized Brain Stimulation to Treat Chronic Concussive Symptoms

skipped_criteria: Mild TBI defined by WHO criteria in the last 12 months: temporal criterion (within 12 months of injury) cannot be expressed with available schema fields or operators.
High burden of post-concussive symptoms (Rivermead Post-Concussion Symptoms Questionnaire score >=20): requires a clinical assessment score not available in the schema.
Objective neurologic deficits: no schema field captures neurologic deficit status.
Ongoing or prolonged (>3 months) post-concussive symptoms from a prior mTBI within 2 years of the index injury: requires prior injury history and temporal logic not available in the schema.
History of transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) therapy: requires prior treatment history not available in the schema.
Contraindications for TMS or MRI (e.g., metallic implant, pacemaker): requires implant/device history not available in the schema.

Severe mental, physical, or medical problems (liver/kidney/heart disease, uncontrolled diabetes or hypertension, malignancy, psychosis, previous seizure): requires medical history fields not available in the schema.
Active alcohol or illicit drug abuse: requires substance use history not available in the schema.
Inability to speak and read English: requires language ability assessment not available in the schema.
